idea.hook: Which planet could FLOAT in water? 🤯
facts: Earth's average density: ~5.51 g/cm³ (5x denser than water)
Jupiter's average density: ~1.33 g/cm³ (denser than water)
Saturn's average density: ~0.687 g/cm³ (LESS dense than water!)
idea.payoff: Saturn is the ONLY planet in our solar system less dense than water. It would literally float!
idea.title: Saturn: The Planet That Floats?! 🛁🪐

scenes.text: Which planet could FLOAT in water? 🤯
scenes.visual: Dark cosmic background with subtle stardust. A large, stylized question mark appears and glows softly.
A large, clear tank of water. An Earth globe descends into the water and visibly sinks to the bottom.
The Earth globe is replaced by a Jupiter globe. It descends into the water and also sinks, landing on top of where Earth was.
The Jupiter globe is replaced by a Saturn globe with prominent rings. It descends towards the water, then gently bobs and floats on the surface.

scenes.text: Saturn: The ONLY planet less dense than water. It would literally float!
scenes.visual: A majestic Saturn (with rings) is shown floating serenely in a vast, calm cosmic ocean, bathed in soft light. Text pulses slightly.
